body:
Classic New Zealand Beef Mince & Cheese Pie

Few dishes are as iconic in New Zealand as the humble mince and cheese pie. Whether picked up from a local bakery on a road trip or baked fresh at home, it's a comforting classic that never goes out of style.

Making your own pie is surprisingly simple and allows you to use quality ingredients while creating enough for the whole family.

Ingredients

Filling

500g premium beef mince
1 onion, finely diced
2 cloves garlic, crushed
2 tbsp tomato paste
1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
1 cup beef stock
1 tbsp cornflour mixed with 2 tbsp water
Salt and pepper
1 tsp dried thyme
1 cup grated tasty cheese

Pastry

2 sheets puff pastry
1 sheet shortcrust pastry
1 egg, beaten
Method
1. Cook the filling

Heat a splash of oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.

Cook the onion until softened before adding the garlic.

Add the beef mince and cook until browned, breaking it apart as it cooks.

Stir through the tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, thyme and beef stock.

Allow the mixture to simmer for around 10 minutes.

Mix the cornflour with water and stir it into the mince until the filling becomes thick and rich.

Season with salt and pepper.

Allow the filling to cool before assembling the pie.

2. Assemble

Line a pie dish with the shortcrust pastry.

Spoon in half the mince mixture.

Sprinkle over half the grated cheese.

Repeat with the remaining mince and cheese.

Cover with puff pastry and press the edges firmly together.

Trim excess pastry and brush the top with beaten egg.

Cut a few small steam vents into the pastry.

3. Bake

Bake at 200°C for approximately 35–40 minutes, or until the pastry is golden and crisp.

Allow the pie to rest for 10 minutes before slicing.

Serving Suggestions

Serve with:

Tomato sauce
Garden salad
Steamed vegetables
Creamy mashed potatoes
Fries for the ultimate bakery-style meal
FoodVault Tip

Many independent New Zealand brands offer premium sauces, cheeses, spices and pantry staples that can elevate simple recipes like this one.

Browsing FoodVault is a great way to discover local producers while enjoying exclusive member offers on ingredients you'll actually use.
meta_description: Learn how to make a traditional New Zealand mince and cheese pie with flaky pastry, rich beef filling and melted cheese. A classic Kiwi family favourite.
meta_title: Classic New Zealand Beef Mince & Cheese Pie Recipe | FoodVault
summary: A true Kiwi favourite, the classic mince and cheese pie is found everywhere from local bakeries to family dinner tables. This homemade version combines rich beef mince, savoury gravy, melted cheese and flaky pastry to create a comforting meal that's perfect for lunch, dinner or entertaining. Pair it with a fresh salad or tomato sauce for a simple, satisfying meal.
title: Classic New Zealand Beef Mince & Cheese Pie

New This Week: Welcome to Olly's Pretzels

One of the best parts of being a FoodVault member is discovering new brands.

Every week, we welcome businesses offering great products and exclusive member savings, giving you even more ways to save while exploring brands you may not have discovered before.

This week, we're excited to welcome Olly's Pretzels to FoodVault.

A Brand Built on Bold Flavour

Olly's began with a simple mission: create snacks that people genuinely love to eat.

What started with marinated olives sold at local farmers' markets has grown into an internationally recognised snack brand, known for combining quality ingredients with bold, memorable flavours.

Today, Olly's is best known for its delicious Pretzel Thins and flavour-packed olive snacks, enjoyed by snack lovers around the world.

Pretzel Thins That Deliver on Taste

Olly's Pretzel Thins have become a favourite for good reason.

Light, crunchy and packed with flavour, they're perfect for:

Lunchboxes
Afternoon snacks
Entertaining
Road trips
Sharing with family and friends

With a range of delicious flavours to choose from, there's something to suit almost every snack lover.

Discover Exclusive Member Offers

As a FoodVault member, you can visit the Olly's brand profile to see their latest member offer and browse their featured products.

FoodVault connects you directly with participating brands, allowing you to discover exclusive member savings while purchasing directly from the brand's own website.

Discover More Every Week

Olly's is just one of the many brands joining FoodVault.

As our community continues to grow, members will regularly discover new businesses, exclusive offers and exciting products across food, beverages and household categories.

Be sure to check back often—you never know what you'll discover next.

body:
Meet Our Partner: Olly's Pretzels

Behind every product is a story, and few are as enjoyable as the journey behind Olly's.

Founded by Olly, the business started with a simple idea: make olives more exciting. Experimenting with marinades in his parents' kitchen, Olly began selling his creations at local farmers' markets. The response was overwhelmingly positive, giving him the confidence to turn his passion into a full-time business.

It was a bold decision. Instead of pursuing a career in medicine, Olly chose to follow his entrepreneurial dream and build a food brand focused on flavour, fun and quality.

From Kitchen Table to International Shelves

As demand grew, Olly was joined by his brother Sam and close friend Yingdee, helping transform a small market stall into a rapidly growing snack business.

The company's first olive snack pouches quickly found their way into retailers across the United Kingdom and overseas. Their popular Garlic & Basil Olives even became the official olive snack served on several airlines, introducing the brand to travellers around the world.

What began as a simple idea was becoming something much bigger.

More Than Just Olives

With their olives proving such a success, the team began exploring new ways to bring exciting flavours to snack lovers.

After years of developing new ideas, two products stood out:

Flavour-packed Olives
Crunchy Pretzel Thins

Today, these have become the heart of the Olly's range, offering bold flavours and satisfying snacks that are perfect for sharing—or keeping all to yourself.

Feeding Your Feel Good

One message has remained at the centre of everything Olly's creates:

Feed Your Feel Good.

While the team believes in creating snacks with quality ingredients, their biggest priority is simple:

Great taste.

Every product is designed to deliver bold flavour and an enjoyable snacking experience, because delicious food is what keeps customers coming back.

A Growing Team

What started with one person in a family kitchen has grown into a passionate team working from their headquarters in London's vibrant Brick Lane.

Along the way they've built a loyal community of snack lovers, affectionately known as the FAM-OLLY, and continue creating new products that bring fun, flavour and personality to the snack aisle.

Discover Olly's on FoodVault

We're delighted to welcome Olly's to FoodVault.

Members can visit the Olly's brand profile to discover their latest member offers, browse featured products and connect directly with the brand through their official website.

Whether you're already a fan of their famous Pretzel Thins or looking to try their delicious olives for the first time, Olly's is a great example of the innovative brands you'll discover on FoodVault.
meta_description: Discover the story behind Olly's Pretzels, from humble beginnings at local farmers' markets to an internationally recognised snack brand known for bold flavours, olives and Pretzel Thins.
meta_title: Meet Our Partner: Olly's Pretzels | FoodVault
summary: Every great brand has a story, and Olly's is one built on passion, persistence and a love of creating seriously tasty snacks. What began with marinated olives prepared on a kitchen table has grown into an internationally recognised snack brand, known for its bold flavours, Pretzel Thins and naturally delicious olives.
title: Meet Our Partner: Olly's Pretzels

Save More Every Week with FoodVault

For many households, finding ways to reduce everyday expenses has become more important than ever. The good news is that saving money doesn't have to mean giving up your favourite products or compromising on quality.

FoodVault was created to help New Zealand shoppers discover participating brands offering exclusive member savings, giving members another way to reduce the cost of everyday purchases.

Start with the Products You Already Buy

One of the easiest ways to save is by focusing on products you already use regularly.

Whether you're shopping for:

Food
Beverages
Household essentials

Many participating brands offer unique products and exclusive member offers that aren't widely advertised.

Exploring new brands can introduce you to quality products while helping you stretch your budget further.

This gives you quick access to:

Keeping your favourites organised makes it easier to shop smarter over time.

Every purchase is completed directly on the partner's own website, helping brands build direct relationships with their customers while giving members access to exclusive savings.

Small Savings Become Bigger Savings

Saving a few dollars on one purchase may not seem significant, but consistent savings throughout the year can quickly add up.

By making FoodVault part of your shopping routine, you can:

Discover participating brands.
Access exclusive member offers.
Save on products you already buy.
Explore new businesses across New Zealand.
Make Saving a Habit

The simplest way to spend less is to make checking FoodVault part of your weekly routine.

Browse participating brands, discover new offers, save your favourites and enjoy exclusive member pricing—all in one place.

Every small saving is another step towards spending smarter.

body:
A Dessert That Never Goes Out of Style

Apple crumble has been a favourite in New Zealand homes for generations. It's simple, affordable and makes the most of fresh seasonal apples. Whether served after a Sunday roast or enjoyed on a cool winter evening, it's a dessert that's guaranteed to bring everyone back for seconds.

Best of all, it uses pantry staples that many households already have on hand.

Ingredients
Apple Filling
6–8 medium apples, peeled and sliced
2 tablespoons brown sugar
1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
1 tablespoon lemon juice
Crumble Topping
1½ cups plain flour
1 cup rolled oats
¾ cup brown sugar
125g butter, chilled and diced
1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
Method
Preheat your oven to 180°C.
Place the sliced apples into a greased baking dish.
Mix the brown sugar, cinnamon, vanilla and lemon juice through the apples.
In a separate bowl, combine the flour, oats, brown sugar and cinnamon.
Rub the butter into the dry ingredients until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Sprinkle the crumble evenly over the apples.
Bake for 40–45 minutes, or until the topping is golden and the apples are tender.

Allow the crumble to cool for a few minutes before serving.

Serving Suggestions

Apple crumble is delicious served with:

Vanilla ice cream
Fresh whipped cream
Warm custard
Greek yoghurt for a lighter option

It's just as enjoyable the next day, gently reheated in the oven or microwave.

Tips for the Perfect Crumble
Use a mix of sweet and tart apples for extra flavour.
Keep the butter cold to achieve a crisp, crumbly topping.
Add chopped walnuts or almonds for extra crunch.
Sprinkle a little brown sugar over the top before baking for an extra golden finish.
Save More on Baking Ingredients

Recipes like apple crumble use everyday pantry staples such as flour, oats, butter, sugar and spices. FoodVault helps members discover participating brands offering exclusive member savings on many of the ingredients you already use at home.

Before your next baking day, browse FoodVault to discover new brands and exclusive member offers.
meta_description: Make a traditional New Zealand apple crumble with tender baked apples and a golden buttery topping. An easy homemade dessert that's perfect served with ice cream, cream or custard.
meta_title: Classic New Zealand Apple Crumble Recipe | FoodVault
summary: Warm, comforting and easy to make, apple crumble is one of New Zealand's most loved homemade desserts. Sweet baked apples topped with a buttery golden crumble make the perfect finish to any family meal. Serve with vanilla ice cream, cream or custard for a timeless Kiwi favourite.
title: Classic New Zealand Apple Crumble

At FoodVault, we connect members directly with participating brands offering exclusive member pricing, helping you discover great products while reducing the cost of the food and household items you already buy.

Here are a few simple strategies to help you get more value from every grocery dollar.

Buy Direct From Brands When It Makes Sense

FoodVault brings these participating brands together in one place, making it easy to discover new favourites and access exclusive member offers.

Plan Before You Shop

body:
A New Zealand Café Classic

Cheese scones have been a staple in New Zealand cafés for decades. Crispy on the outside, soft and fluffy inside, they're best enjoyed straight from the oven with plenty of butter while still warm.

Unlike sweeter baking recipes, cheese scones are savoury, making them a versatile option for breakfast, lunch, or afternoon tea.

Ingredients
3 cups self-raising flour
1 teaspoon salt
1 teaspoon mustard powder (optional)
2 cups grated tasty cheese
50g cold butter
1¼ cups milk
Method
Preheat your oven to 220°C.
Mix the flour, salt and mustard powder in a large bowl.
Rub the butter into the flour until it resembles breadcrumbs.
Stir through most of the grated cheese, reserving a small handful for the top.
Add the milk and mix gently until just combined.
Turn onto a lightly floured bench and pat into a rectangle about 3cm thick.
Cut into squares or triangles and place on a baking tray.
Sprinkle the remaining cheese over the top.
Bake for 15–18 minutes until golden brown.
Tips for Perfect Cheese Scones
Don't overmix the dough—this keeps the scones light.
Use strong, aged cheddar for the best flavour.
Serve immediately while warm.
Freeze leftovers and reheat in the oven for a quick snack.
Make Them Your Own

Try adding:

Chopped spring onions
Fresh chives
Cracked black pepper
Diced ham
Jalapeños for a spicy twist
Save More on Everyday Ingredients

Many of the ingredients used in recipes like this—including flour, butter, cheese and pantry staples—are available from participating FoodVault brands offering exclusive member discounts. Checking FoodVault before you shop is an easy way to discover new brands while saving on everyday essentials.
meta_description: Bake classic New Zealand cheese scones at home with this easy recipe. Discover simple ingredients, baking tips, and ways to save on everyday pantry staples with FoodVault.
meta_title: Homemade Cheese Scones | Classic New Zealand Recipe | FoodVault
summary: Few things are more Kiwi than a warm cheese scone fresh from the oven. Whether paired with butter, soup, or a hot coffee, cheese scones are a favourite in cafés across New Zealand. They're simple to make, use everyday ingredients, and are perfect for lunchboxes, picnics, or weekend baking.
title: Homemade Cheese Scones

body:
A Kiwi Favourite for Every Occasion

Bacon and egg pie has earned its place as one of New Zealand's most loved comfort foods. Whether packed into a chilly bin for a summer picnic or served fresh from the oven at brunch, it's a recipe that generations of Kiwis have grown up enjoying.

One of the best things about bacon and egg pie is its simplicity. With just a handful of ingredients, you can create a hearty meal that's perfect for sharing.

Ingredients
2 sheets flaky puff pastry
8 eggs
250g streaky bacon, chopped
1 small onion, finely diced (optional)
2 spring onions, sliced
2 tablespoons chopped parsley
Salt and freshly cracked black pepper
1 egg, beaten (for glazing)
Method
Preheat your oven to 190°C.
Line a pie dish with one sheet of puff pastry, allowing it to overlap the edges.
Scatter the chopped bacon evenly over the base.
Add the onion, spring onions and parsley.
Carefully crack the eggs over the filling, spacing them evenly throughout the pie.
Season lightly with salt and pepper.
Cover with the second sheet of pastry and press the edges together.
Trim any excess pastry and crimp the edges with a fork.
Brush the top with beaten egg and cut a few small slits to allow steam to escape.
Bake for 40–45 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and crisp.

Allow the pie to rest for 10 minutes before slicing.

Serving Suggestions

Bacon and egg pie is delicious served with:

Fresh green salad
Tomato relish
Beetroot chutney
Tomato sauce
Seasonal fruit for a picnic

It can also be enjoyed cold, making it perfect for lunchboxes, road trips or meal preparation.

Tips for the Best Bacon & Egg Pie
Use quality free-range eggs for a richer flavour.
Avoid overfilling the pie so the pastry stays crisp.
Let the pie cool slightly before slicing to help it hold its shape.
Add spinach or mushrooms for extra flavour and texture.
Save on Everyday Ingredients

Many of the ingredients used in this recipe—including bacon, eggs, pastry, herbs and pantry staples—can often be found through participating FoodVault brands offering exclusive member savings.

Before planning your next family meal, explore FoodVault to discover participating brands and exclusive offers that can help you save while trying great New Zealand products.
meta_description: Make a traditional New Zealand bacon and egg pie with flaky pastry, crispy bacon and perfectly baked eggs. A simple family favourite that's perfect for brunch, lunch or picnics.
meta_title: Classic Kiwi Bacon & Egg Pie Recipe | FoodVault
summary: A true New Zealand favourite, bacon and egg pie is a staple at family gatherings, picnics, school lunches and weekend brunches. With flaky pastry, crispy bacon and perfectly baked eggs, it's simple to prepare and always a crowd-pleaser. Serve it warm or cold for an easy meal any time of the day.
title: Classic Kiwi Bacon & Egg Pie
